title: Thelypteris monosora
intro: Thelypteris monospora, commonly known as the American maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris monospora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris monospora is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used in the form of tinctures and extracts to leverage its health benefits.
taxonomy: Thelypteris monosora is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris and the phylum Pteridophyta. Within the kingdom Plantae, Thelypteris monosora is further classified as a vascular plant. The classification of Thelypteris monosora is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, phylum - Pteridophyta, class - Polypodiopsida, order - Thelypterida, family - Thelypteridaceae, genus - Thelypteris, species - T. monosora. This classification system is widely used in botany to organize and identify plant species.
